title: V3Const BitOpTree optimization is incorrect
body:
I've got 2 designs that trigger bug with BitOpTree optimization in V3Const.
[test.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/8772881/test.zip)
They are called `pack_struct_bug` and `pack_struct_2bug`.

Both of this designs use array of structs.
There is also `pack_struct_different_order` design that uses packed struct, but order of fields is different and bug is not triggered.
`verilator --version : Verilator 4.223 devel rev v4.222-11-gf6035447`

I'm running Debian Linux docker container for testing.

Main issue here is that `ConstBitOpTreeVisitor` visitor going down expresion tree does not keep tract of previous nodes it had seen.
Path like `AstAnd -> AstShiftR -> AstAnd -> AstArraySel` created fail as AstArraySel is not handled. This would make `AstArraySel` subtree as frozen node and information of `AstShiftR` before `AstArraySel` would be lost. This is a case in `pack_struct_bug`.
In `pack_starct_2bug`path is `AstAnd -> AstNot -> AstShiftR -> AstAnd -> AstArraySel`. In this case both `AstNot` and `AstShiftR` nodes are lost.

I don't know best solution to this issue but I've came up with a fix. 
It is rather crude as it may disable optimization in some cases that it's not needed.
[BiOpFix.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/8773009/BiOpFix.txt)

[edit]
I've tested how this affects speed of simulation. The way it's right now, simulation was 3 times slower.
